name: Bitrix24 Open Chanel RAG Chatbot Application Workflow example with Webhook Integration
description: Automated workflow: Bitrix24 Open Chanel RAG Chatbot Application Workflow example with Webhook Integration. This workflow integrates 22 different services: stickyNote, textSplitterRecursiveCharacterTextSplitter, vectorStoreQdrant, chainRetrievalQa, merge. It contains 57 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

options.systemPromptTemplate:
=Use the following pieces of context to answer the user's question.
If you don't know the answer, just say that you don't know, don't try to make up an answer.

----------------
{context}

Your response must contain **only** the following key-value pairs:
- `"DIALOG_ID"`: **Use exactly** this value from the input: `{{ $json.DIALOG_ID }}`
- `"AUTH"`: **Use exactly** this value from the input: `{{ $json.AUTH }}`
- `"DOMAIN"`: **Use exactly** this value from the input: `{{ $json.DOMAIN }}`
- `"MESSAGE"`: **Your AI-generated response**, based on the conversation history and the user's input.

**Do not modify** the values of `"DIALOG_ID"`, `"AUTH"`, or `"DOMAIN"`. They must remain exactly as received from the input.  
The `"MESSAGE"` field must contain a relevant and clear response.

If the user asks **"find out more about me"**, respond with:  
*"I am a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system that answers questions based on uploaded documents and provided context."*

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: No custom credentials required for utility-only operations.
configurationSteps: Locate the webhook node named "Bitrix24 Handler".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Locate the webhook node named "Success Response".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Locate the webhook node named "Error Response".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Verify the settings of the trigger node "Execute Workflow Trigger".
Open the "Validate Token" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Route Event" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Process Message" node (n8n-nodes-base.function) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Process Join" node (n8n-nodes-base.function) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Process Install" node (n8n-nodes-base.function) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Register Bot" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send Message" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send Join Message" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Process Delete" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Merge parameters for Subworkflow" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get a list of available storages" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get a list of List of Files and Folders" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get a list of Folders files" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Download file" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Default Data Loader" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Recursive Character Text Splitter" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split Out folder files and folders" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Filter for files" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Move files to Vector stored folder" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Qdrant Vector Store" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Embeddings Ollama" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Vector Store Retriever" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Question and Answer Chain" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Embeddings Ollama1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Gemini Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Qdrant Vector Store1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Execute subworkflow" node (n8n-nodes-base.executeWorkflow) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Credentials".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Prepare output parameters".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
